subject: Computer Networking
title: The Browser Wars
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Browser Wars.

hook.text: Netscape Navigator vs. Internet Explorer. The battle for the browser defined the mid-1990s tech landscape.

buildup.text: Netscape launched in 1994 and dominated quickly. Microsoft bundled IE with Windows in 1995 to compete.

discovery.text: Both browsers added features at breakneck speed — JavaScript, CSS, plugins — often incompatibly.

twist.text: Microsoft's bundling strategy worked. IE reached 95% market share by 2002. Netscape collapsed.

climax.text: Netscape open-sourced its code before dying. That code became Firefox, which reignited browser competition.

punchline.text: Netscape lost the war but lit the spark for open-source browsers.

quiz.question: What happened to Netscape's code after the company declined?
options: It was open-sourced and became the basis for Firefox
It was sold to Apple for Safari
